name: Steel Porta Cabin
description:
Steel Porta Cabin — MS, GI, and Pre-Galvanised Options

When a buyer searches for a steel porta cabin in India, they are almost always describing a mild steel cabin — because MS construction accounts for more than 90% of the porta cabin market in India. But steel is not one material in this context. It is three distinct specifications, and the right one depends entirely on where your cabin will be deployed.

When "Steel" Means Three Different Things — MS, GI, and Pre-Galvanized

Understanding what each term means before ordering prevents a specification mismatch that is expensive to correct after delivery.

Mild Steel (MS): Hot-rolled low-carbon steel conforming to IS 2062. The structural frame — base channels, corner columns, roof members — is fabricated from MS plate and sections. Wall and roof panels are colour-coated MS corrugated sheet. MS is the most common, most widely available, and most economical steel specification in Indian porta cabins. It requires a correct factory surface treatment to resist corrosion. Correctly treated and maintained, an MS cabin lasts 20 to 25 years.

Standard MS corrugated steel porta cabin at an active industrial site — open-front view showing workstation interior, LED lighting, and side-access door. Grey colour-coated corrugated steel panels with standard structural frame. Most Indian construction site porta cabins use this MS configuration.

Galvanised Iron (GI): Steel coated with a zinc layer through a hot-dip galvanizing process. The zinc sacrificially protects the underlying steel from corrosion without requiring paint — even if the surface is scratched. GI costs 15 to 20% more than equivalent MS construction. It is specified for deployments where the surface treatment warranty on MS is considered insufficient — coastal environments, chemical-adjacent sites, or long-duration high-humidity deployments.

Pre-galvanized (Zincalume / Galvalume): Cold-rolled steel sheet coated with a zinc-aluminium alloy before fabrication — applied at the steel mill, not at the cabin factory. Used for wall cladding and roof sheets only, not for structural frame members. Pre-galvanized cladding has better corrosion resistance than colour-coated MS sheet and costs 8 to 12% more than the standard MS cladding option. It is not the same as hot-dip GI and is not a substitute for GI frame specification.

Which Steel Type for Which Site — A One-Line Recommendation Per Deployment

Inland construction site, dry climate (Rajasthan, UP, Madhya Pradesh): Standard MS with factory surface treatment. Most economical, adequate for 3 to 5 year deployments. No GI premium justified.

Coastal site (Kerala, Goa, coastal Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu): Pre-galvanized wall and roof cladding on an MS structural frame. Delivers improved panel corrosion resistance at 8 to 12% premium — without the full GI cost. Most common upgrade specification for South and West Coast deployments.

High-humidity inland site (Assam, Meghalaya, dam site, dense forest location): GI structural frame with pre-galvanized panels. Maximum corrosion protection. Justified when the cabin will remain at a remote, difficult-to-maintain location for 5 or more years.

A steel porta cabin in a high-humidity forest setting — the deployment environment where GI frame or pre-galvanised cladding is justified over standard MS. Warm interior confirms a fully fitted and operational cabin.

Industrial or chemical environment (near processing plants, fertilizer sites, battery storage): GI frame confirmed. Chemical-adjacent environments accelerate MS corrosion regardless of surface treatment quality.

Standard construction site, any inland location, 2 to 4 year deployment: MS with correct surface treatment. Adequate and most cost-effective. Repainting every 2 to 3 years maintains full lifespan.

For buyers who have confirmed MS is the right specification and want the full IS 2062 Grade A frame detail — dimensions, load capacity, and surface treatment process — our ms porta cabin page covers the complete material specification.

Price Difference — What Each Steel Type Costs Relative to Standard

MS specification is the baseline price. Every SAMAN steel porta cabin price quoted at the order stage is for MS construction unless otherwise specified.

Pre-galvanized cladding upgrade (wall and roof panels only, MS frame retained): 8 to 12% premium over equivalent standard MS cabin. For a 10×20 ft cabin, this adds approximately Rs 12,000 to Rs 18,000 to the standard MS price.

Full GI specification (GI frame, GI or pre-galvanized panels): 15 to 20% premium over equivalent MS cabin. For a 10×20 ft cabin, this adds approximately Rs 22,000 to Rs 35,000.

In most inland Indian deployments, the MS surface treatment specification delivers adequate corrosion protection at the lowest cost. The pre-galvanised cladding upgrade is the most cost-effective corrosion protection step for coastal and high-humidity sites. Full GI is reserved for sites where maintenance access is very limited or chemical corrosion risk is confirmed.

What SAMAN Supplies in Each Steel Configuration

The structural geometry of the cabin is identical across all three steel specifications. Base frame section profiles, room dimensions, door and window positions, roof pitch — all unchanged. Only the material changes.

Standard MS configuration: IS 2062 Grade A mild steel structural frame throughout. Colour-coated corrugated MS wall and roof sheets. Every MS cabin receives a multi-coat factory surface treatment before dispatch — for the full surface treatment specification including primer type, topcoat process, and 20-year lifespan maintenance schedule, see our ms porta cabin page.

Pre-galvanised cladding upgrade: IS 2062 Grade A mild steel structural frame unchanged. Zincalume or Galvalume corrugated sheet replacing standard colour-coated MS for wall cladding and roof — applied without additional paint coating.

Steel porta cabin with pre-galvanised corrugated cladding in olive green at a remote hillside deployment — solar panel on roof confirms off-grid capability. Interior workspace visible through open sliding glass door. Pre-galvanised wall and roof sheets on MS structural frame: 8–12% premium over standard MS, best value corrosion upgrade for remote and coastal deployments.

Full GI configuration: Hot-dip galvanized structural frame members and GI or pre-galvanized wall and roof sheets. No paint required on frame — zinc coating provides native protection. Factory-applied epoxy topcoat available for colour finish.

For buyers evaluating the most economical MS specification — entry-level price, standard construction, standard surface treatment — our low-cost porta cabin page covers budget configurations.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I specify a hybrid configuration — GI structural frame members with standard MS wall panels?

Yes. SAMAN can fabricate a hybrid configuration with GI base frame and corner columns combined with standard colour-coated MS corrugated wall and roof sheets — the most structurally vulnerable members get GI protection while panel replacement is easier and cheaper with standard MS. This hybrid costs approximately 8 to 10% more than a full MS cabin and is available on request at the quote stage.

Does the choice between MS, GI, and pre-galvanised affect government procurement eligibility in India?

No. All three steel specifications are eligible for government procurement. Government tender documents typically specify the material as "MS or equivalent" — GI and pre-galvanised both qualify. If your tender specifies IS 2062, confirm at order stage that the structural frame uses IS 2062 Grade A MS regardless of the cladding specification chosen.

How do I identify whether a cabin I already own uses MS, GI, or pre-galvanised panels?

MS panels have a painted surface — if the paint has chipped and you see red-orange rust on the exposed steel, it is standard MS. GI panels have a characteristic spangled zinc surface visible at cut edges even under paint — no red rust forms, white zinc oxide forms instead. Pre-galvanised Zincalume sheet has a distinctive fine grey-silver spangle pattern visible at cut or scratched edges and does not rust red.

Tell us your site location, deployment duration, and humidity level.

We will confirm the right steel specification — MS, pre-galvanised upgrade, or full GI — and provide a factory price within 24 hours. Delivery from our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities across India in 7 to 21 working days.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
